title: Some Thingss
description: Some Things is a mod that does not add new blocks or items. It updates existing mechanics, making them deeper and more interesting
body:
# Some Things Mod
**Some Things** is a Vanilla+ mod that aims to breathe new life into familiar Minecraft mechanics. The mod does not add new ores or dimensions, but makes existing items and interactions deeper and more logical.

**_Mod Menu_**: An in-game configuration menu (default key 'N') allows you to toggle any individual feature on or off to suit your playstyle

**Features:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

Quality of Life (QoL)

    Eternal Anvil: Completely removes the vanilla "Too Expensive" limit when repairing or enchanting.

    Fast Leaf Decay: Leaves decay 5x faster when the last log of a tree is broken.

    Double Doors: Opening one door automatically opens the adjacent connected door (Shift + Right-Click to open just one).

    Faster Ladders: Looking down while descending a ladder significantly increases your descent speed.

    Stackable Soups: Soups and stews can now be stacked up to 8 items per slot.

    Heavy Recycling: Dropping an anvil onto armor or tools "flattens" them, refunding half of their crafting resources.

Combat, Tools & Armor

    Golden Authority: Wearing a full set of Gold Armor makes Piglins neutral, doubles their bartering speed, and slightly increases rare loot chances.

    Stealth in Leather: A full set of Leather Armor prevents you from triggering Sculk vibrations while walking.

    Light Chainmail: Wearing Chainmail armor grants a bonus to Bow and Crossbow drawing/firing speed.

    Precision Shearing: Shears can be enchanted with Fortune to drop more wool from sheep.

    Arthropod Scourge: The Bane of Arthropods enchantment allows you to instantly mine and walk through cobwebs.

    Thunderstrike: A Trident with Channeling can summon lightning in any weather, provided you are standing in water near an active Conduit.

    Tactical Spyglass: Observing an enemy through a spyglass for more than 3 seconds marks them with the Glowing effect.

    Combat Torch: Hitting an entity with a torch has a 25% chance to set them on fire.

    Useful Snowballs: Thrown snowballs now extinguish fires and apply a Slowness effect to enemies.

Farming & Food

    Auto-Planting: Right-clicking a fully grown crop with a hoe instantly harvests and replants the seeds.

    Crop Protection: Players wearing Leather Boots will no longer trample farmland by jumping on it.

    Flesh Recycling: Rotten Flesh can be smelted on a Campfire or in a Smoker to produce Leather.

    Food Buffs: Consuming Cookies, Pumpkin Pies, and Stews now grants minor, temporary buffs.

    Rainy Growth: All plants grow 20% faster while it is raining.

Mobs & Animals

    Ground Feeding: Cows, pigs, sheep, and chickens can eat their favorite dropped food items from the ground to heal or enter breeding mode.

    Scavenger Pigs: Pigs can consume Rotten Flesh and Poisonous Potatoes to breed (does not restore health).

    Pack Hunter: Tamed wolves deal bonus damage for every other tamed wolf attacking the same target.

    Squid Defense: The ink cloud from a damaged squid applies Blindness (3-5 seconds) to any entity caught inside.

Transportation

    Trains: Minecarts can now be chained together by right-clicking them with a Chain.

    High-Speed Tracks: Rails placed on top of Gravel allow minecarts to travel significantly faster.

    Furnace Carts: Furnace Minecarts now feature a custom GUI with 9 slots for fuel and items.

World & Loot

    Biome-Specific Loot: Chest loot generation is now heavily influenced by the biome you are currently in.

    Logical Potions: The Potion of Night Vision has a new, alternative crafting recipe using a Glow Ink Sac

</details>
